examples.translation: The sun is shining today.
We like the warm sun.
Protect yourselves from the sun.
I want to buy milk.
Did you buy the tickets?
We are buying fruits from the market.
I ate a red apple.
I like green apples.
Do you want an apple?
This is a big house.
He has a large dog.
The city is very big.
I am Yannis.
I didn't do it.
Who said it? I (did)!
I am reading an interesting book.
Where is my book?
I bought three books.
I like running in the park.
Why are you running?
We were running to catch the bus.
The sea is blue.
Let's go to the sea!
I swim in the sea every day.
The fish swims in the sea.
I like to eat grilled fish.
They caught a very big fish.

notes: The plural form is τα ψάρια (ta psária).

examples.translation: He is my brother.
This is the house.
I spoke with him.

notes: Changes form for gender and case (αυτός/αυτή/αυτό).

examples.translation: I want some water, please.
The water is cold.
I drink a lot of water.
I am writing a letter.
I am writing to inform you.
He wrote a book.
Athens is a large city.
I am going to the city.
I live in the city.
This is a small car.
He has a little dog.
The town is very small.
You are Maria.
You don't know it.
Who said it? You (did)!
My cat is black.
I see a cat in the garden.
I like cats.
I speak Greek.
Why aren't you talking to me?
We want to speak with the manager.
There is a big tree in the garden.
I am sitting under the tree.
The trees are green in the summer.
This is a beautiful city.
He is a very pretty child.
I like beautiful music.
I want to go, but I can't.
It is expensive, but good.
It is not blue, but green.
My dog is very friendly.
We saw a big dog in the park.
You have to feed the dog.
The chair is very comfortable.
Sit on this chair.
We need four chairs for the table.

examples.greek: Το <mark>σπίτι</mark> μου είναι κοντά στη θάλασσα.
examples.translation: My house is near the sea.
Let's go home now.
They bought a big house.
The mountain is covered with snow.
We like to walk in the mountains.

examples.greek: Η θέα από το <mark>βουνό</mark> είναι φανταστική.
examples.translation: The view from the mountain is fantastic.
Put some oil in the salad.
Greek olive oil is excellent.
I need oil for frying.
I ate a sweet pomegranate.
We break a pomegranate on New Year's Day.

examples.greek: Ο χυμός από <mark>ρόδι</mark> είναι πολύ υγιεινός.
examples.translation: Pomegranate juice is very healthy.
The boy is playing football.
He is a smart boy.
I saw two boys in the park.
The man is reading the newspaper.

examples.greek: Αυτός ο <mark>άνδρας</mark> είναι ο δάσκαλός μου.
examples.translation: This man is my teacher.
Her husband works at the bank.
I love my family very much.
They have a big family.
The family eats together every Sunday.
The girl is wearing a red dress.

examples.greek: Εκείνο το <mark>κορίτσι</mark> είναι η αδερφή μου.
examples.translation: That girl is my sister.
The girls are reading books.
My grandma makes a nice pie.
We are going to see grandma tomorrow.

examples.greek: Αυτή είναι μια φωτογραφία της <mark>γιαγιάς</mark> μου.
examples.translation: This is a photo of my grandmother.
The woman is driving the car.

examples.greek: Μίλησα με τη <mark>γυναίκα</mark> που δουλεύει εκεί.
examples.translation: I spoke with the woman who works there.
His wife is a doctor.
My son goes to school.

examples.greek: Είμαι πολύ περήφανος για τον <mark>γιο</mark> μου.
examples.translation: I am very proud of my son.
They have two sons.
My father works hard.
I want to speak to your father.

examples.greek: Οι <mark>πατέρες</mark> παίζουν με τα παιδιά τους.
examples.translation: The fathers are playing with their children.
Her daughter is studying law.

examples.greek: Αγόρασα ένα δώρο για την <mark>κόρη</mark> μου.
examples.translation: I bought a gift for my daughter.
They have one daughter and one son.
My mother is a teacher.
I bought flowers for my mother.
Mothers love their children.
I read a book every night.
What are you reading now?
I like to read on the beach.
Shall we go to the beach for a swim?

examples.greek: Η <mark>παραλία</mark> ήταν γεμάτη κόσμο το καλοκαίρι.
examples.translation: The beach was full of people in the summer.

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει να περπατάω στην <mark>παραλία</mark> το πρωί.
examples.translation: I like to walk on the beach in the morning.
Yes, I want a coffee, please.
Did you say yes to my proposal?
Yes, I am from Greece.
Do you want coffee? No, thank you.
Is this true? No, it's not.

examples.greek: Μπορείς να έρθεις; <mark>Όχι</mark>, δεν μπορώ σήμερα.
examples.translation: Can you come? No, I can't today.
You made a big mistake.

examples.greek: Αυτό είναι ένα συχνό <mark>λάθος</mark> που κάνουν οι μαθητές.
examples.translation: This is a common mistake students make.
It's okay, making a mistake is human.

examples.greek: Σας <mark>ευχαριστώ</mark> πολύ για τη βοήθειά σας.
examples.translation: Thank you very much for your help.
Thank you for the gift, it's wonderful!

examples.greek: Θέλω να σας <mark>ευχαριστήσω</mark> όλους για την παρουσία σας.
examples.translation: I want to thank you all for your presence.

notes: Regular verb, often used impersonally as 'ευχαριστώ' (thank you).

examples.translation: Can you help me, please?
Thank you very much! - You're welcome!

examples.greek: Τι <mark>παρακαλώ</mark>; Πώς μπορώ να σας εξυπηρετήσω;
examples.translation: What can I do for you? How can I help you?

notes: Literally means 'I beg' or 'I request'. Commonly used as 'please' or 'you're welcome'.

examples.translation: I want a piece of bread.
The bread is fresh today.
I bought bread from the bakery.
I have a good friend in Athens.
He is my best friend.

examples.greek: Θα πάω βόλτα με τον <mark>φίλο</mark> μου.
examples.translation: I will go for a walk with my friend.
He is a good friend.
She has a good heart.
This is a good book.
Good morning! Have a wonderful day.
Today's day is sunny.
How many days will you stay in Greece?
Good night everyone!

examples.greek: Η <mark>νύχτα</mark> ήταν ήσυχη και γεμάτη αστέρια.
examples.translation: The night was quiet and full of stars.

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει να διαβάζω ένα βιβλίο τη <mark>νύχτα</mark>.
examples.translation: I like to read a book at night.
The river is very long.
We swam in the river yesterday.

examples.greek: Η πόλη βρίσκεται δίπλα σε έναν <mark>ποταμό</mark>.
examples.translation: The city is located next to a river.
The abstract painting has many colors.

examples.greek: Η <mark>αφηρημένη</mark> ιδέα είναι δύσκολο να εξηγηθεί.
examples.translation: The abstract idea is difficult to explain.
The abstract design is very modern.

examples.greek: Η <mark>ευτυχία</mark> είναι το πιο σημαντικό πράγμα στη ζωή.
examples.translation: Happiness is the most important thing in life.
I wish you much happiness!
He found happiness in simplicity.

examples.greek: Η <mark>αγάπη</mark> είναι το πιο δυνατό συναίσθημα.
examples.translation: Love is the strongest feeling.

examples.greek: Έχω μεγάλη <mark>αγάπη</mark> για την οικογένειά μου.
examples.translation: I have great love for my family.

examples.greek: Η <mark>αγάπη</mark> για τη μάθηση είναι σημαντική.
examples.translation: The love for learning is important.
The drama was very moving.

examples.greek: Θα πάμε να δούμε ένα καινούργιο <mark>δράμα</mark> στο θέατρο.
examples.translation: We will go to see a new play at the theater.
Her life is full of drama.
I have a new car.
The car is parked outside.
We want to buy an electric car.
I have a new bicycle.

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει να κάνω <mark>ποδήλατο</mark> στην εξοχή.
examples.translation: I like to ride a bike in the countryside.

examples.greek: Το <mark>ποδήλατο</mark> είναι ένα οικολογικό μέσο μεταφοράς.
examples.translation: The bicycle is an ecological means of transport.
I eat breakfast every day.

examples.greek: Το <mark>πρωινό</mark> είναι το πιο σημαντικό γεύμα.
examples.translation: Breakfast is the most important meal.
We will go for coffee in the morning.

examples.greek: Το <mark>δείπνο</mark> είναι έτοιμο, ελάτε να φάτε.
examples.translation: Dinner is ready, come and eat.

examples.greek: Θα πάμε για <mark>δείπνο</mark> σε ένα ωραίο εστιατόριο απόψε.
examples.translation: We will go for dinner at a nice restaurant tonight.

examples.greek: Είχαμε ένα υπέροχο <mark>δείπνο</mark> με φίλους χθες το βράδυ.
examples.translation: We had a wonderful dinner with friends last night.
The meal was very delicious.

examples.greek: Τρώμε το <mark>γεύμα</mark> μας στις δύο το μεσημέρι.
examples.translation: We eat our lunch at two in the afternoon.

examples.greek: Ετοιμάζω ένα ελαφρύ <mark>γεύμα</mark> για το βράδυ.
examples.translation: I am preparing a light meal for the evening.
The coffee is very tasty.
The salad is delicious.
The food was tasty.
I like to eat fried potatoes.

examples.greek: Η <mark>πατάτα</mark> είναι ένα πολύ κοινό λαχανικό.
examples.translation: The potato is a very common vegetable.

examples.greek: Θα ήθελα μια <mark>πατάτα</mark> ψητή με βούτυρο.
examples.translation: I would like a baked potato with butter.

examples.greek: Θα φάμε μια νόστιμη <mark>πίτα</mark> για μεσημεριανό.
examples.translation: We will eat a delicious pie for lunch.

examples.greek: Μπορείτε να μου δώσετε μια <mark>πίτα</mark> για τον γύρο;
examples.translation: Can you give me a pita for the gyros?

examples.greek: Η σπανακό<mark>πιτα</mark> είναι η αγαπημένη μου.
examples.translation: Spinach pie is my favorite.
I want a little pepper in my salad.
Black pepper is very aromatic.

examples.greek: Μπορείς να μου περάσεις το <mark>πιπέρι</mark>, παρακαλώ;
examples.translation: Can you pass me the pepper, please?
The plate is empty.

examples.greek: Μπορείτε να μου φέρετε ένα καθαρό <mark>πιάτο</mark>;
examples.translation: Can you bring me a clean plate?

examples.greek: Έπλυνε όλα τα <mark>πιάτα</mark> μετά το δείπνο.
examples.translation: He washed all the dishes after dinner.
The horse runs fast in the meadow.

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει να βλέπω το <mark>άλογο</mark> να τρώει γρασίδι.
examples.translation: I like to watch the horse eat grass.
Horses are very beautiful animals.
The animal is sleeping in the garden.
I have a pet animal, a dog.
Many animals live in the forest.
I want a coffee, please.
Greek coffee is very strong.
I drink coffee every morning.
I drink water every morning.
Do you want to drink a coffee together?

examples.greek: Το μωρό <mark>πίνει</mark> το γάλα του γρήγορα.
examples.translation: The baby drinks its milk quickly.
I want a lemon for the salad.
The lemon is sour.

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει ο χυμός από φρέσκα <mark>λεμόνια</mark>.
examples.translation: I like juice from fresh lemons.
I have a hat.
The hat is red.
I want to buy a new hat.

examples.greek: Χρειάζομαι ένα ζευγάρι <mark>γάντια</mark> για τον χειμώνα.
examples.translation: I need a pair of gloves for winter.
My glove fell down.
I wear gloves when I wash the dishes.

notes: Neuter noun. The plural form is γάντια.

examples.translation: The duck swims in the lake.
We saw many ducks in the park.

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει το ψητό <mark>πάπια</mark> με πορτοκάλι.
examples.translation: I like roast duck with orange.
The bird is singing in the tree.
I see a beautiful bird in the sky.
The birds fly high.
Reptiles are cold-blooded animals.
I don't like reptiles.

examples.greek: Είδα ένα μικρό <mark>ερπετό</mark> στον κήπο μας.
examples.translation: I saw a small reptile in our garden.
I want to buy a new shirt.
The blue shirt is very nice.
He is wearing a white shirt today.
Her skirt is very beautiful.
I want to buy a new skirt.
She is wearing a red skirt today.

examples.greek: Αγόρασα μια καινούργια <mark>μπότα</mark> για το χειμώνα.
examples.translation: I bought a new boot for winter.
Her boots are very elegant.
I need waterproof boots for hiking.
I want a peach for dessert.

examples.greek: Το <mark>ροδάκινο</mark> είναι ένα από τα αγαπημένα μου φρούτα.
examples.translation: The peach is one of my favorite fruits.
I bought fresh peaches from the market.
My sister lives in Athens.
Do you have a sister?
She is my younger sister.

examples.greek: Θέλω λίγο <mark>τυρί</mark> για τη σαλάτα μου.
examples.translation: I want a little cheese for my salad.
Greek cheese is very delicious.

examples.greek: Μπορώ να έχω ένα σάντουιτς με <mark>τυρί</mark>;
examples.translation: Can I have a cheese sandwich?
I lost my wallet at the market.

examples.greek: Μέσα στο <mark>πορτοφόλι</mark> του είχε μόνο κέρματα.
examples.translation: Inside his wallet, there were only coins.
This wallet is very practical.
The mouse is small.
I saw a mouse in the kitchen.
The cat caught the mouse.
The pelican is a large bird.
We saw many pelicans in the lake.
The pelican caught a fish.
The turtle swims slowly in the water.
We have a small turtle as a pet.
Sea turtles are a protected species.
I want to buy a new t-shirt.
Your t-shirt is very nice.
He/She is wearing a red t-shirt today.
The eagle flies high in the sky.
I saw a big eagle on the mountain.
The eagle is a strong bird.
I like to eat crab.
I saw a big crab on the beach.
Crabs live in the sea.
The police officer was wearing his uniform.

examples.greek: Τα παιδιά στο σχολείο φορούν σχολική <mark>στολή</mark>.
examples.translation: The children at school wear a school uniform.

examples.greek: Για το καρναβάλι, θα φορέσω μια αστεία <mark>στολή</mark>.
examples.translation: For carnival, I will wear a funny costume.
His skin is very soft.
I bought a bag made of genuine leather.

examples.greek: Πρέπει να προστατεύουμε το <mark>δέρμα</mark> μας από τον ήλιο.
examples.translation: We must protect our skin from the sun.
A beautiful butterfly flew in the garden.

examples.greek: Τα παιδιά κυνηγούσαν την <mark>πεταλούδα</mark> με το δίχτυ.
examples.translation: The children were chasing the butterfly with the net.
The caterpillar transforms into a butterfly.
The <mark>new</mark> student is very smart.
The <mark>new</mark> teacher speaks Greek.
The <mark>new</mark> car is red.

examples.greek: Ο <mark>τελευταίος</mark> πελάτης έφυγε από το μαγαζί.
examples.translation: The last customer left the store.

examples.greek: Αυτή είναι η <mark>τελευταία</mark> μου ευκαιρία να μιλήσω.
examples.translation: This is my last chance to speak.

examples.greek: Το <mark>τελευταίο</mark> κεφάλαιο του βιβλίου ήταν συναρπαστικό.
examples.translation: The last chapter of the book was exciting.
The first student entered the classroom.

examples.greek: Αυτή είναι η <mark>πρώτη</mark> φορά που ταξιδεύω.
examples.translation: This is the first time I travel.

examples.greek: Το <mark>πρώτο</mark> πράγμα που κάνω το πρωί είναι να πίνω καφέ.
examples.translation: The first thing I do in the morning is drink coffee.
The road is long.
The story is long.
The river is long.

notes: Adjective with three endings (μακρύς, μακριά, μακρύ).

examples.translation: Time passes very quickly.
How much time do you need for this?
Every year we visit our grandparents.
I have a fear of heights.
Fear is a strong emotion.

examples.greek: Μην αφήνεις τον <mark>φόβο</mark> να σε σταματάει.
examples.translation: Don't let fear stop you.

examples.greek: Έχω ένα <mark>πρόβλημα</mark> με τον υπολογιστή μου.
examples.translation: I have a problem with my computer.

examples.greek: Αυτό είναι ένα σοβαρό <mark>πρόβλημα</mark> που πρέπει να λύσουμε.
examples.translation: This is a serious problem that we need to solve.
There are no problems, only solutions.

examples.greek: Έχω μια καλή <mark>ιδέα</mark> για το πάρτι.
examples.translation: I have a good idea for the party.
Your idea is excellent!

examples.greek: Μου ήρθε μια νέα <mark>ιδέα</mark> για το έργο.
examples.translation: A new idea came to me for the project.
I like music.
She listens to music every day.
The music is loud.
I have a new phone.

examples.greek: Μπορείς να μου δώσεις το <mark>τηλέφωνό</mark> σου;
examples.translation: Can you give me your phone number?
The phone is ringing loudly.
I like milk chocolate.
Do you want a piece of chocolate?
I bought a bar of chocolate.
I want a cucumber for the salad.
The cucumber is refreshing.
I like cucumbers with yogurt.
The room is in absolute chaos.
After the accident, chaos prevailed.

examples.greek: Το μυαλό του ήταν ένα <mark>χάος</mark> από σκέψεις.
examples.translation: His mind was a chaos of thoughts.

examples.greek: Η <mark>Ακαδημία</mark> είναι ένα ιστορικό κτίριο στην πόλη.
examples.translation: The academy is a historic building in the city.
She studies at the Academy of Fine Arts.
The academy offers many interesting courses.
The government announced new measures.

examples.greek: Η νέα <mark>κυβέρνηση</mark> θα ξεκινήσει τον επόμενο μήνα.
examples.translation: The new government will start next month.
The people voted for a new government.
My company is big.
He works for a new company.
The company offers good services.
What is your phone number?
A large number of people were there.
Can you write the number here?
Our team won the game.
We are a good group at work.

examples.greek: Θα φτιάξουμε μια <mark>ομάδα</mark> για το έργο.
examples.translation: We will form a team for the project.

examples.greek: Αυτό είναι ένα σημαντικό <mark>γεγονός</mark> για την πόλη μας.
examples.translation: This is an important event for our city.

examples.greek: Το να βλέπεις την ανατολή του ηλίου είναι ένα όμορφο <mark>γεγονός</mark>.
examples.translation: Watching the sunrise is a beautiful event.

examples.greek: Είναι ένα γνωστό <mark>γεγονός</mark> ότι η Ελλάδα έχει πολλή ιστορία.
examples.translation: It is a known fact that Greece has a lot of history.
The food is very delicious today.

examples.greek: Ετοιμάζω το <mark>φαγητό</mark> για όλη την οικογένεια.
examples.translation: I am preparing the meal for the whole family.
I like Greek food.
I will answer your question tomorrow.
Why don't you answer my messages?
We must answer the challenge.
I have a question for you.
Can I ask a question?
The question is very interesting.
Their wedding was very beautiful.
We are preparing our daughter's wedding.
Marriage is a sacred union.
The puppy is playing in the garden.
I want to adopt a puppy.
Puppies are very cute.

examples.greek: Ο <mark>ελέφαντας</mark> είναι ένα πολύ μεγάλο ζώο.
examples.translation: The elephant is a very large animal.
I saw an elephant at the zoo.
Elephants have a long trunk.
I always wear comfortable clothes.
She is wearing a beautiful dress today.
What are you wearing for the party?

examples.greek: Το <mark>παλτό</mark> είναι πολύ ζεστό για τον χειμώνα.
examples.translation: The coat is very warm for winter.
Can I try on this coat?
Leave your coat on the hanger.
I want a glass of wine.
The red wine is very good.
We drink wine with dinner.
I saw an insect in the garden.
There are many insects in the summer.
This insect is very small.

examples.greek: Το <mark>βρέφος</mark> κοιμάται ήσυχα στην κούνια του.
examples.translation: The baby is sleeping peacefully in its crib.
A newborn infant needs a lot of care.
The baby smiled at its mother.
I want new trousers.
Your pants are very nice.
I bought blue trousers.
The wolf is a wild animal.
We saw a wolf in the forest.
Wolves hunt in packs.
I want an avocado for the salad.
Avocado is very healthy.
I like avocado toast.
She is my teacher.
I saw this movie yesterday.
I spoke to her about the trip.

notes: Feminine demonstrative pronoun/adjective. 'Αυτήν' is the accusative singular form.

examples.greek: Εγώ <mark>δένω</mark> τα κορδόνια μου κάθε πρωί.
examples.translation: I tie my shoelaces every morning.

examples.greek: Μπορείς να <mark>δέσεις</mark> το σχοινί στην κολόνα;
examples.translation: Can you tie the rope to the pole?

examples.greek: Θα <mark>δέσει</mark> το πακέτο πριν το στείλει.
examples.translation: He will tie the package before he sends it.
I want a chocolate ice cream.
The ice cream is very delicious.
Shall we go for ice cream after dinner?

examples.greek: Μου αρέσει να τρώω <mark>φρούτα</mark> το πρωί.
examples.translation: I like to eat fruit in the morning.
This fruit is very sweet.
Do you want a fruit for dessert?
The bear lives in the forest.

examples.greek: Είδα μια μεγάλη <mark>αρκούδα</mark> στο ζωολογικό κήπο.
examples.translation: I saw a big bear at the zoo.
Bears eat honey.
The tiger is a wild animal.
I saw a tiger at the zoo.
Tigers are big cats.

examples.greek: Η <mark>ζέβρα</mark> έχει μαύρες και άσπρες ρίγες.
examples.translation: The zebra has black and white stripes.
We saw a zebra in the documentary.
Zebras are wild animals.

examples.greek: Το δελφίνι είναι ένα <mark>θηλαστικό</mark> που ζει στη θάλασσα.
examples.translation: The dolphin is a mammal that lives in the sea.
All mammals have a spine.
Humans are also mammals.
The giraffe has a very long neck.
We saw a giraffe at the zoo.
Giraffes are impressive animals.
